subject: Re: St-James - Library
body:
the library is 30 not forty feet wide

the library is 30 not forty feet wide

--- jpm6929@aol.com wrote:

Dear Jeffrey;
Sorry you didn' receive this before but I had
problems with my mail. I hope now is working.
The building is 120 feet by 40, plus 15 and 15 the
Antichambre and the Cabinet next to the entrance
vestibule. The volumes work beautifully,
considering that the entrance vestibule is double
hight, and above the office is the sitting/reading
room. The centre, the Cabinet is much lower. This
gives us the more classical of proportions , I will
send it as soon as I finish it. Placing the
building towards the flag pole has some
complications, as the terrain slopes down all the
way almost up to the pole.I imagine that you have
allready
scaned all that sector. Let me now.J.P.

subject: Re: St-James - Library
body:
THis fights with every axis... gives me no place at
all to sit and admire our favorite view from inside
..through the window we discussed,, THe office has the
premier view thorugh two narrow north facing windows
and has a big window facing my house.... this makes no
sense whatsover

--- jpm6929@aol.com wrote:

Dear Jeffrey;
Sorry you didn' receive this before but I had
problems with my mail. I hope now is working.
The building is 120 feet by 40, plus 15 and 15 the
Antichambre and the Cabinet next to the entrance
vestibule. The volumes work beautifully,
considering that the entrance vestibule is double
hight, and above the office is the sitting/reading
room. The centre, the Cabinet is much lower. This
gives us the more classical of proportions , I will
send it as soon as I finish it. Placing the
building towards the flag pole has some
complications, as the terrain slopes down all the
way almost up to the pole.I imagine that you have
allready
scaned all that sector. Let me now.J.P.

subject: Re: St-James - Library
body:
please  DON"T SEND ME THINGS without a full topo,,
entry road idea, and total island aerial... I 'm not
good enough to decipher,, where do the bikes go, where
is the shade.. where does the wind go. how do we
define the precinct
